title: Data update
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"airr","text":"This protocols aims to details the steps necessary to update previously collected data points for the \"Database Justice League\" project.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4034o","text":"Before starting this protocol, you need to have recieved the list of articles you will be working on. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"857cp","text":"Contact the professor if you haven't received the dataset.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Once you have received the link to download your dataset, download the csv file locally. Make sure to save it on your computer in a convenient folder! In this protocol we will perform the data collection on a test dataset: DB_DataUpdate_TD.csv

As you can see in this test dataset, some preliminary information have been collected automatically such as the database name, link and availability in 2016 However some information will need additional efforts to be collected, such as the current availability of the database.
In the dataset, you'll find a column name "current_access". This is the latest link known for the database. In order to access the database, copy and paste this link in your browser.
We know that this ressource was available in 2016. Now, we need to assess if the URL for the database is still working. Click on the URL you identified! Two possibilities: The URL is working or not!

Great job! You collected all the data we needed to complete the DB Justice League! You should now have a table filled as follow: DB_DataUpdate_TD_filled.csv Upload the table in your github repo and notify us of the upload! Thank you for your great contribution!
creator: Alise J Ponsero
